title: Stream claude -p output via stream-json for real-time delegation events
source: Claude Code SDK docs: --output-format stream-json / --include-partial-messages (knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; live web search unavailable this run)
description: delegate.py likely blocks on full subprocess completion before emitting results, so the Rick Terminal and roro stream only update when a Morty finishes. Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json with --include-partial-messages, emitting newline-delimited JSON events (message_start, content_block_delta, tool_use, result) as work happens. Consuming this gives live per-token/per-tool progress, partial-failure detection, and accurate cost/duration from the final result event.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py, invoke claude with `-p --output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages` and read stdout line-by-line, json.loads each line. Map event types to roro emissions in roro_events.py (tool_use -> 'morty.tool', content_block_delta -> 'morty.token', result -> 'morty.done' with total_cost_usd/duration_ms/num_turns). Add a parser that tolerates non-JSON lines and surfaces the final result event for ticket review. Keep the old buffered path behind a --no-stream flag for sleepy/batch runs.
